title: narcissus rupicola subsp. auricolor
intro: Narcissus rupicola subsp. auricolor, a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Narcissus rupicola, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as galantamine. The health benefits of Narcissus rupicola subsp. auricolor are largely anecdotal, but it is believed to have neuroprotective and vasodilatory effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as Alzheimer's disease and high blood pressure. Herbal preparations of Narcissus rupicola subsp. auricolor can be made by infusing the plant's flowers and bulbs in water or alcohol, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and improve cognitive function.
